varieties.name: OB1
varieties.origin: Bred in Northern NSW, Australia, released 2019
varieties.traits: Southern Highbush, very low chill (150-250 hours); Semi-evergreen, grows 1-2m high, compact habit, extremely low-chill requirement
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity, fruits almost year-round in humid subtropical climates

varieties.name: Sunshine Blue
varieties.origin: University of Florida breeding program, USA, 1990s
varieties.traits: Southern Highbush, very low chill (150-200 hours); Compact dwarf variety (1-1.5m), semi-evergreen, attractive blue-green foliage turning burgundy in fall
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity, reliable cropping

varieties.name: Misty
varieties.traits: Southern Highbush, low chill (300-400 hours); Semi-evergreen, upright vigorous growth, 1.5-2m height
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity, early season ripening (late spring)

varieties.name: Biloxi
varieties.traits: Southern Highbush, very low chill (200-300 hours, tolerates up to 40°C); Vigorous, upright-spreading growth, semi-evergreen, heat tolerant
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy producer, often has second crop later in season
varieties.growing_notes: EXCELLENT for humid subtropical climates. Exceptional heat tolerance (up to 40°C). Can produce two crops per year in humid subtropical climates. Early to mid-season. Best pollinated with Misty or Sharp Blue. Very reliable subtropical performer.
varieties.availability: Fruit Tree Lane, Ladybird Nursery, major QLD suppliers

varieties.name: Sharp Blue (Sharpblue)
varieties.traits: Southern Highbush, very low chill (150-200 hours); Upright spreading bush, 1.5-2m, nearly evergreen in warm climates
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity, can fruit year-round in mild climates

varieties.name: Gulf Coast
varieties.traits: Southern Highbush, low chill (300-400 hours); Productive, hardy shrub, vigorous, good field survival
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity, reliable
varieties.growing_notes: EXCELLENT for humid subtropical climates. Self-fertile (can plant solo). Hardy and easy to grow. Early season ripening. Highly recommended for beginners. Recommended by Fruit Tree Lane as top choice for Glass House Mountains/SE QLD.
varieties.availability: Fruit Tree Lane, major humid subtropical climates/QLD nurseries

varieties.name: Emerald
varieties.traits: Southern Highbush, very low chill (200-300 hours); Vigorous, stout semi-erect canes, 1.5-2m, semi-evergreen
varieties.yield_notes: Very high yields, maintains large berry size throughout harvest
varieties.growing_notes: EXCELLENT for humid subtropical climates. Prolific production. Was backbone of Florida industry for decade. Early season. Cross-pollinate with Star, O’Neal, or Jewel. Reliable humid subtropical climates performer. Commercial quality.

varieties.name: Star
varieties.origin: University of Florida, USA, patented selection
varieties.traits: Southern Highbush, low chill (300-450 hours); Upright-spreading habit, good spring leaf development, attractive gray-green foliage with red stems
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity
varieties.growing_notes: GOOD for humid subtropical climates. Part of Florida industry backbone. Late April/mid-May ripening in warm climates. Requires cross-pollination (O’Neal, Santa Fe, Emerald). Susceptible to Septoria leaf spot. May be marginal in warmer humid subtropical climates areas.

varieties.name: O’Neal
varieties.origin: North Carolina State University, USA, introduced 1987
varieties.traits: Southern Highbush, moderate chill (450 hours); Vigorous, bushy growth habit, 1.2-1.8m, gray-green foliage with red stems
varieties.yield_notes: High yield, consistent quality throughout harvest
varieties.growing_notes: MARGINAL for warmest humid subtropical climates areas. Excellent flavor. Usually first to ripen. Requires pollinator (Star compatible). May struggle in very low-chill areas but worth trying in cooler humid subtropical climates suburbs. Can leaf poorly in insufficient chill.

varieties.name: Blueberry Burst®
varieties.traits: Southern Highbush, low chill (300-400 hours); Naturally dwarfing, evergreen, compact bushy habit, 1-1.5m
varieties.yield_notes: High yield over 4-6 months
varieties.growing_notes: EXCELLENT for humid subtropical climates. Australian-bred specifically for local conditions. Self-pollinating. Perfect for containers. When planted with Blueberry Kisses®, provides 6-8 months supply. Favorite at Brookfield Gardens.
varieties.availability: Brookfield Gardens humid subtropical climates, PlantNet Australia stockists

varieties.name: Blueberry Kisses®
varieties.traits: Southern Highbush, low chill (300-400 hours); Naturally dwarfing, evergreen, less bushy than Burst, 1-1.5m
varieties.yield_notes: Exceptionally high yield, harvest over 3-4 months
varieties.growing_notes: EXCELLENT for humid subtropical climates. Australian-bred. Characterized by extra-large fruit size. Self-pollinating but best with Blueberry Burst®. Achieves huge fruit through traditional breeding. All Australian climates except frost-prone under -4°C.

varieties.name: Brightwell 🧬 (Rabbiteye)
varieties.traits: Rabbiteye, moderate chill (350-400 hours); Vigorous, upright growth, highly adaptable, beautiful red fall foliage
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy producer (15+ pounds/bush)
varieties.growing_notes: EXCELLENT for humid subtropical climates. One of most reliable rabbiteyes. Drought tolerant. Heat adaptable. Top performer in humid subtropical climates climate. Partially self-pollinating but plant with Powderblue or Tifblue for best results. Recommended for Perth and humid subtropical climates.
varieties.availability: Fruit Tree Lane, major QLD nurseries

varieties.name: Powderblue (Powder Blue) 🧬 (Rabbiteye)
varieties.origin: North Carolina State University/USDA-ARS, USA, released 1978
varieties.traits: Rabbiteye, moderate chill (500-650 hours); Vigorous, upright growth with good foliage, 2-3m height, deciduous
varieties.yield_notes: Highly productive, late season

varieties.name: Nelly Kelly (Ned Kelly)
varieties.traits: Likely Southern Highbush or Rabbiteye hybrid, low to moderate chill; Vigorous, hardy, well-adapted to humid subtropical conditions
varieties.yield_notes: Productive, fruits over extended period (to March)

varieties.name: Legacy
varieties.origin: USDA New Jersey, USA, developed by USDA Research Station
varieties.traits: Northern Highbush (but adaptable), moderate chill (550-800 hours); Upright vigorous growth, attractive fall color
varieties.yield_notes: Highly productive, late season

varieties.name: Biloxi
varieties.traits: Southern Highbush, zero to very low chill (150-300 hours, tolerates up to 40°C); Vigorous, heat-tolerant, semi-evergreen, upright growth
varieties.yield_notes: Very high productivity in tropical climates
varieties.growing_notes: EXCELLENT for tropical monsoon climates. Top choice for tropical India. Specifically tolerates temperatures up to 40°C. Recommended by Neva Plantations for tropical monsoon climates climate. Can produce in continuous warm conditions. Best performer in Indian tropical regions.
varieties.availability: Neva Plantations (delivers tropical monsoon climates), Greens of tropical monsoon climates, Indian Glorious Nursery

varieties.name: Emerald
varieties.traits: Southern Highbush, very low chill (200-300 hours, performs up to 38°C); Vigorous, stout semi-erect canes, semi-evergreen, good heat adaptation
varieties.yield_notes: Very high yields, reliable in warm climates
varieties.growing_notes: EXCELLENT for tropical monsoon climates. Recommended by Neva Plantations. Requires only 250 chill hours. Large, flavorful berries. Performs well in temperatures up to 38°C. Good for both home gardens and commercial production in tropical monsoon climates.
varieties.availability: Neva Plantations (tropical monsoon climates delivery), tropical monsoon climates online nurseries

varieties.name: Misty
varieties.traits: Southern Highbush, moderate chill (300-400 hours, performs up to 38°C); Upright vigorous growth, semi-evergreen, adaptable
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity, reliable bearer
varieties.growing_notes: GOOD for tropical monsoon climates (may need some protection in hottest months). Recommended by Neva Plantations for warmer tropical monsoon climates regions. Reliable tropical performer with 400 chill hours. Balance of cold tolerance and heat adaptability. Moderate climate zones.
varieties.availability: Neva Plantations, Indian nurseries delivering to tropical monsoon climates

varieties.name: Sharp Blue
varieties.traits: Southern Highbush, very low to no chill (150-200 hours); Upright spreading, nearly evergreen in warm climates, vigorous
varieties.yield_notes: Productive, versatile, late bearing extends season

varieties.name: Sunshine Blue
varieties.origin: University of Florida breeding program, USA, 1990s
varieties.traits: Southern Highbush, very low chill (150-200 hours); Compact dwarf (1-1.5m), semi-evergreen, tolerates higher pH, ornamental
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity, reliable

varieties.name: Robin Blue
varieties.traits: Northern Highbush, high chill (700-1200 hours); Vigorous growth, requires cooler conditions
varieties.yield_notes: Productive in cool climates
varieties.growing_notes: NOT SUITABLE for tropical monsoon climates. High chill requirements (700-1200 hours) make this completely unsuitable for tropical tropical monsoon climates climate. Mentioned by Neva Plantations for reference only. Better for hill stations like Munnar, not lowland tropical monsoon climates/tropical monsoon climates.
varieties.availability: Not recommended for tropical monsoon climates lowlands

varieties.name: Vaccinium corymbosum (General Highbush)
varieties.origin: Native to North America, various cultivated varieties
varieties.traits: Highbush blueberry species, variable chill depending on cultivar; Deciduous to semi-evergreen shrub, 1-2m height, dark green glossy leaves
varieties.yield_notes: Variable depending on cultivar and conditions

varieties.name: Kerala Blueberry (Marketing Name)
varieties.origin: Variety uncertain - marketed name for tropical-adapted selection
varieties.traits: Likely Southern Highbush or adapted selection, ultra-low chill; Marketed as suitable for tropical/temperate tropical monsoon climates climate
varieties.yield_notes: Variable

varieties.name: Blue Berry (Tropical Variety)
varieties.origin: Variety uncertain - marketed as tropical variety
varieties.traits: Marketed as tropical-adapted, likely Southern Highbush, ultra-low chill; Sold as adapted to tropical Indian conditions
varieties.yield_notes: Claims good production in tropical areas

varieties.name: Jersey 🏆 HEIRLOOM
varieties.traits: Northern Highbush, high chill (800-1000 hours); Late season, vigorous, very cold hardy, deciduous, 1.2-1.8m
varieties.yield_notes: Very high productivity, reliable bearer

varieties.name: Bluecrop 🏆 HEIRLOOM
varieties.origin: USDA New Jersey, USA, developed 1934, introduced 1941
varieties.traits: Northern Highbush, high chill (800-1000 hours); Most widely planted variety in USA, very hardy, upright spreading, 1.2-1.8m
varieties.yield_notes: Very high, consistent, reliable (over 25% Michigan acreage)

varieties.name: Duke
varieties.traits: Northern Highbush, high chill (800-1000 hours); Early season, upright vigorous, blooms late but ripens early, cold hardy
varieties.yield_notes: High yield, reliable, can live 50+ years

varieties.name: Elizabeth 🏆 HEIRLOOM
varieties.origin: New Jersey Blueberry Council, USA, selected 1966, re-introduced 2002
varieties.traits: Northern Highbush, high chill (800+ hours); Named after Elizabeth White (blueberry pioneer), spreading habit, vigorous, fast growing, 1.8m
varieties.yield_notes: High yield, 10-12 lbs per bush, one of most vigorous

varieties.name: Tifblue 🏆 HEIRLOOM (Rabbiteye)
varieties.traits: Rabbiteye, moderate chill (500-650 hours); Mid-season, vigorous upright growth, burgundy red fall foliage, deciduous
varieties.yield_notes: Very high (up to 15 lbs/plant), most productive rabbiteye

varieties.name: Climax 🏆 HEIRLOOM (Rabbiteye)
varieties.traits: Rabbiteye, moderate chill (450-550 hours); Early season, vigorous upright, easy to grow, orange-red fall foliage
varieties.yield_notes: Very high (up to 25 lbs/plant), heavy producer

varieties.name: Premier 🧬 (Rabbiteye)
varieties.traits: Rabbiteye, moderate chill (500-650 hours); Early to mid-season, upright growth with good foliage, vigorous
varieties.yield_notes: Highly productive, reliable
varieties.growing_notes: POSSIBLE for cooler humid subtropical climates areas, NOT SUITABLE for tropical monsoon climates. One of ‘old standard’ rabbiteyes alongside Climax, Tifblue, Brightwell, Powderblue. Excellent flavor noted. Plant with Brightwell or Tifblue as pollinators. Top 5 yielder in LSU trials (12.2 lbs/plant).
